buggy_proof.description: Uses one shared k for two odd numbers (forces a=b)
buggy_proof.notes: If translator fixes the bug (introduces k,l), fidelity should drop. Type A requires Lean-verified counterexample.

correct_proof.description: Valid proof that sum of two odd integers is even
correct_proof.notes: Should have high fidelity and no Type A errors. All gaps should be Type B (auto-resolved) or Type D (Lean limitation).

buggy_proof.description: Delta Method variance error: uses g'(1) instead of [g'(1)]^2, gets N(0,e) then hand-waves to N(0,e^2)
buggy_proof.notes: The bug is in step 6-7: Delta Method gives variance [g'(mu)]^2 * sigma^2, not g'(mu) * sigma^2. The hand-wave in step 7 is a non-sequitur attempting to cover the error. Type A should flag the incorrect variance computation. Expect Type C/D gaps for CLT and Delta Method formalization in Lean.

correct_proof.description: Valid proof: product of i.i.d. Uniform[0,1] via log-transform, CLT, and Delta Method
correct_proof.notes: Multi-step probability proof: log-transform -> Exp(1) -> CLT -> Delta Method. Should have high fidelity. Expect Type C (Mathlib gaps for CLT/Delta Method) and Type D (measure-theoretic API) sorry gaps, but NO Type A logical errors.
